steps.title: Spot the opening dash and what it marks
steps.content: An opening dash after “temperature” marks an appositive phrase, “around 225 degrees Fahrenheit.” After this phrase comes the independent clause, “flaxseed oil will begin to emit smoke.”

steps.title: Apply the matching dashes rule
steps.content: A pair of matching dashes must enclose parenthetical elements. Choice B adds the required closing dash after Fahrenheit. The other options either omit the closing dash or use incorrect punctuation marks. That matches choice B — B is correct.
